mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe demand contraction, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy above 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during periods of monetary tightening signal supply-side constraints that outweigh liquidity support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect broad economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60-day return direction with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly jumps above 0.5 percent are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market absorption, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 20-day return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero interest rates combined with stable inflation indicate accommodative monetary conditions that sustain equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in over 55 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent.
